queued_publication_packets.title: Does every 2-colored cocktail party graph admit a cover by two monochromatic diameter-2 subsets?
queued_publication_packets.publication_packet_title: Two monochromatic diameter-2 covers in 2-colored cocktail party graphs
queued_publication_packets.publication_if_solved: Solving the original diameter-2 cocktail-party conjecture would read as a direct short follow-up to the 2026 relaxed theorem.
queued_publication_packets.why_this_could_be_publishable: It is a direct upgrade from a very recent paper's main relaxed theorem to the original exact conjecture.
queued_publication_packets.canonical_source: Andras Gyarfas and Gabor N. Sarkozy, "2-Reachable Subsets in Two-Colored Graphs" (Graphs and Combinatorics 42, 2026), which states and relaxes the English-McCourt-Mattes-Phillips conjecture.

queued_publication_packets.title: Does the ladder with 4 rungs have the edge-Erdos-Posa property?
queued_publication_packets.publication_packet_title: The edge-Erdos-Posa property for the 4-rung ladder
queued_publication_packets.publication_if_solved: A resolution of the 4-rung ladder case is already a standalone note because the 2024 paper leaves ladders with 4 to 13 rungs explicitly open.
queued_publication_packets.why_this_could_be_publishable: It is a smallest-open-case yes/no theorem with immediate context from the 3-rung positive result and the 14-rung negative barrier.
queued_publication_packets.canonical_source: Raphael Steck and Arthur Ulmer, "On the Edge-Erdos-Posa Property of Ladders" (Graphs and Combinatorics 40, 2024).

queued_publication_packets.title: Is every 9-regular graph on 12 vertices one-factorizable?
queued_publication_packets.publication_if_solved: A complete degree-9 classification on order 12 is already a short exact-classification paper because the source explicitly leaves that slice open.
queued_publication_packets.why_this_could_be_publishable: It is an exact unresolved finite classification with a tiny verifier and essentially zero post-solve packaging tax.
queued_publication_packets.canonical_source: Petteri Kaski and Patric R. J. Ostergard, "One-Factorizations of Regular Graphs of Order 12" (Electronic Journal of Combinatorics 12, 2005).

queued_publication_packets.title: Does every 8-regular graph admit an F-avoiding orientation for every 3-element forbidden set?
queued_publication_packets.publication_packet_title: The d = 8 slice of the forbidden out-degree orientation conjecture
queued_publication_packets.publication_if_solved: A clean d=8 theorem or counterexample would already be a short follow-up paper to the 2026 d<=6 result.
queued_publication_packets.why_this_could_be_publishable: It is the exact next even-degree slice after a fresh 2026 advance, with a ready-made paper narrative and tiny verification footprint.
queued_publication_packets.canonical_source: Owen Henderschedt and Jessica McDonald, "On orientations with forbidden out-degrees" (Discrete Applied Mathematics 387, 2026).

queued_publication_packets.title: Are all graphs on 9 vertices 1-11-representable?
queued_publication_packets.publication_if_solved: Settling the 9-vertex slice would already be a finite-classification note, either as a complete positive census or as a first minimal counterexample paper.
queued_publication_packets.why_this_could_be_publishable: It is exactly the kind of smallest unresolved finite slice that can become a clean note or a minimal-counterexample paper with low novelty-check cost.
queued_publication_packets.canonical_source: Mohammed Alshammari, Sergey Kitaev, Chaoliang Tang, Tianyi Tao, and Junchi Zhang, "On 1-11-representability and multi-1-11-representability of graphs" (Utilitas Mathematica 122, 2025), together with Futorny-Kitaev-Pyatkin, "New Tools to Study 1-11-Representation of Graphs" (Graphs and Combinatorics 40, 2024).
